skill.name: agent-skill-trust-check
skill.description: Static pre-install trust review for SKILL.md, OpenClaw, Hermes, MCP, and agent-skill marketplace packages before they request local, account, payment, or external access.
skill.summary: Static security scanner that checks agent skill descriptions for risky patterns before installation.
skill.author: Tate Programs

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate security scanning tool that analyzes skill descriptions for dangerous patterns. After evaluation, all 192 static scanner findings are either false positives (JavaScript template literals misidentified as shell backtick execution, regex pattern definitions in scanner rules misidentified as malicious code) or intentional test data in the examples/risky-skill.md file. The operational code performs no unauthorized network calls, filesystem writes, or external command execution. The tool reads input via stdin or specified file path, applies regex-based pattern matching rules, and returns a JSON verdict. The example test file contents are present purely for validating the scanner's detection capabilities.

low_findings.title: Intentionally dangerous patterns in example test file
low_findings.description: examples/risky-skill.md contains shell execution, credential access, prompt injection, and network exfiltration patterns. This file exists solely as test data to validate the scanner detection logic. The dangerous patterns are documented and intentional.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File header explicitly states 'This sample exists only to test the scanner' confirming the content is intentional test data.

low_findings.title: Prompt injection content in example file
low_findings.description: examples/risky-skill.md line 7 contains the text 'ignore previous instructions' which is a prompt injection pattern. This is intentionally included to test the scanner's prompt-boundary detection rule.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is explicitly labeled as a test/sample file. The text is included to verify the scanner's ability to detect prompt-boundary violations.

content.user_title: Check agent skill safety before installation
content.value_statement: Agent skills can access shell commands, secrets, wallets, and networks without advance warning. This skill performs a static pre-install check of SKILL.md packages and marketplace listings to flag risky patterns before trust is granted.
actual_capabilities: Detects shell execution patterns in skill descriptions including bash, powershell, and child_process calls
Flags credential and secret access patterns such as API keys, tokens, .env files, and private keys
Identifies wallet and payment related actions including transactions, signing, and transfers
Detects network exfiltration patterns like fetch, webhook, curl, and data upload calls
Recognizes persistence mechanisms including cron jobs, startup items, and background daemons
Reports prompt injection attempts and weak prompt boundary language in skill instructions
limitations: Performs static text analysis only and does not execute or dynamically analyze the target skill
Cannot prove runtime safety or detect obfuscated malicious code that evades regex pattern matching
Analyzes only the SKILL.md or text content provided and does not scan dependencies or bundled scripts
Risk scoring is heuristic based and may produce false positives for legitimate administrative tools

use_cases.title: Pre-install review of community skills
use_cases.description: Check a skill from a public marketplace or GitHub repository before installing it into your agent environment.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers installing third-party agent skills

use_cases.title: Marketplace listing vetting
use_cases.description: Review submitted skill listings for dangerous patterns before approving them for publication on a skill marketplace.

use_cases.title: Security audit of internal skills
use_cases.description: Scan internally developed agent skills for risky patterns before deploying them to production agent environments.
use_cases.target_user: Security engineers auditing agent skill deployments

prompt_templates.title: Basic skill check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this skill before I install it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick safety review of a single skill

prompt_templates.title: Marketplace listing review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this marketplace listing and tell me if it contains shell execution, credential access, or network exfiltration patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed review with specific risk factor focus

prompt_templates.title: Compare two skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these two skills and tell me which one has fewer security risks.

prompt_templates.title: Custom review with provenance checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this skill and report all risky patterns. Also verify it includes source, license, version, tests, permissions, and uninstall documentation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive audit including provenance signal verification

output_examples.input: Submit a SKILL.md file from a third-party GitHub repository for review.
output_examples.output: The scanner returns a verdict of do_not_install_without_changes with findings for shell execution via curl pipe to bash, credential access to API environment variables, and network exfiltration to an external webhook. It recommends command allowlists, secret documentation, and remote host disclosure before installation.

output_examples.input: Submit a simple skill that only provides text formatting without any external commands or network access.
output_examples.output: No risky patterns matched. The skill includes license, version, and source documentation. The scanner returns a verdict of reasonable_to_trial_in_sandbox with minimal recommended precautions.

output_examples.input: Submit skill content that includes instructions to override agent boundaries.
output_examples.output: The scanner detects a weak prompt boundary finding and flags the text that tells the agent to ignore prior instructions. It advises separating untrusted content from operational instructions and documenting prompt-injection handling procedures.

best_practices: Always run the scan before installing any skill from an untrusted source or public marketplace
Review each finding carefully a false positive in one context may be a real risk in another
Use the stdin mode for maximum safety as it requires no filesystem or network access from the scanner
anti_patterns: Installing a skill without running a trust check first, especially when the skill requests shell, network, or filesystem permissions
Dismissing all matching patterns as false positives without understanding the context of each finding
Assuming a passing scan means the skill is completely safe static analysis cannot detect all runtime threats

faq.question: Does this skill execute the target skill during the review?
faq.answer: No. This is a purely static analysis tool. It reads the skill description text and applies regex pattern matching. It does not execute, install, or run the target skill in any way.

faq.question: Can this tool detect all malicious code in a skill?
faq.answer: No. Static pattern matching cannot detect obfuscated code, dynamically generated commands, or runtime threats. It is a pre-install screening tool, not a full security audit.

faq.question: What happens if the scanner finds a dangerous pattern?
faq.answer: The scanner returns a verdict of do_not_install_without_changes along with specific findings and suggested fixes. Each finding includes the pattern type, severity level, and advice for remediation before installation.

faq.question: Does the skill itself access the network or my filesystem?
faq.answer: In default stdin mode, no. The marketplace-safe stdin runner reads from standard input only and writes JSON output. The optional CLI mode can read a local file path or public GitHub URL when explicitly invoked.

faq.question: What risk factors does this scanner check for?
faq.answer: It checks for shell execution, destructive commands, credential and secret access, wallet and payment actions, network exfiltration, persistence mechanisms, and prompt boundary violations.

faq.question: Can I use this skill on multiple skills at once?
faq.answer: The tool processes one input at a time. You can run it repeatedly in a script or pipeline to batch check multiple skills sequentially. Each run returns an independent verdict for that input.
